questions.q:
Statements:
Some Red is Blue
Some Blue is Green
No Green is White
All White is Pink

Which conclusion follows?
opts: Some Blue is not White is a possibility
All Green can never be Pink
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Some Pink can be Blue

Green × White (No relation), All White is Pink so Pink and Green are separate. Blue partially overlaps Green. Since No Green is White, White→Pink is separate from Green. However Blue overlaps White indirectly through Pink is possible. Hence Some Pink CAN be Blue is a possibility — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
All Milk is Coffee
Some Coffee is Tea
No Tea is Juice
Some Juices are Drink

Which conclusion follows?
opts: Some Drinks can never be Juice
Some Drinks can never be Tea
questions.sol:
Answer: E — Some Drinks can never be Tea

No Tea is Juice → No Juice is Tea. Some Juices are Drink, so those Drinks (that are Juice) can NEVER be Tea. Hence Some Drinks can never be Tea — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
No Card is Paper
Some Papers are Plastic
All Plastics are Waste
Some Waste is Carbon

Which conclusion follows?
opts: All Plastics are Card is a possibility
questions.sol:
Answer: A — Some Papers can be Carbon

Some Papers are Plastic → All Plastics are Waste → Some Waste is Carbon. So: Some Papers→Plastic→Waste→possible Carbon. Hence Some Papers CAN be Carbon — TRUE possibility.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Pens are Pencil
All Pencils are Sketch
All Sketch is Crayons
No Crayon is Eraser

Which conclusion follows?
opts: All Pens are Sketch is a possibility
questions.sol:
Answer: D — All Pens are Sketch is a possibility

Some Pens are Pencil → All Pencils are Sketch. It is POSSIBLE that ALL Pens are also Sketch (no statement prevents it). Hence All Pens are Sketch is a possibility — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Books are Note
All Notes are Record
No Record is Project
All Projects are Work

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: B — Some Works are not Note

No Record is Project → All Projects are Work, so Projects are separate from Record/Note. Since Notes→Records and No Record is Project, those Works that ARE Projects are definitely NOT Notes. Hence Some Works are not Note — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Films are Drama
All Drama is Music
Some Concerts are Music
All Concerts are Movie

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: E — Either A or C follows

Films→Drama→Music. Concert→Music and Concert→Movie. Films and Movies may or may not overlap. Either "No Film is Movie" OR "Some Movies are Film" must be true (complementary). Hence Either A or C follows — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
All Remote is TV
All TV is Cable
No Cable is OTT
Some OTT is Cinema

Which conclusion follows?
opts: No Remote is OTT is a possibility
questions.sol:
Answer: D — All Cinema is not TV

Remote→TV→Cable. No Cable is OTT → Some OTT is Cinema, so Cinema is on OTT side. Since No Cable is OTT, and TV⊂Cable, no TV is OTT, no TV is Cinema. Hence All Cinema is not TV — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Classes are Tuition
All Tuitions are College
Some Colleges are University
No University is School

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: C — All University can be Tuition

Tuition⊂College, Some College is University. No statement prevents University from being Tuition. Hence All University can be Tuition is a possibility — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Fans are Heater
No Heater is Trimmer
All Trimmers are Razors
All Razors are Blade

Which conclusion follows?
opts: All Trimmers are Blade is a possibility
questions.sol:
Answer: E — Some Fans can be Blade

Some Fans are Heater. No direct relation to Blade for Fans via Heater. However Fans that are NOT Heater can possibly be Trimmer→Razor→Blade. Hence Some Fans CAN be Blade — TRUE possibility.

questions.q:
Statements:
All Fridges are A/C
Some A/C is Inverter
No Inverter is Oven
All Ovens are Stove

Which conclusion follows?
opts: No Fridge is Stove is a possibility
questions.sol:
Answer: C — No Fridge is Stove is a possibility

Fridge⊂A/C. Some A/C is Inverter, No Inverter is Oven. Fridges that are not Inverters have no connection to Stove. Hence No Fridge is Stove is a possibility — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Dealers are Agent
Some Agents are Officer
All Officers are Manager
No Manager is Clerk

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: B — Some Clerks can be Agent

No Manager is Clerk. Some Agents are Officer→Manager. Agents that are NOT Officers have no relation with Clerk — so it is possible some Clerks are also those Agents. Hence Some Clerks can be Agent — TRUE possibility.

questions.q:
Statements:
All Branches are Bank
Some Banks are Office
All Offices are Complex
Some Complexes are Apartment

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Some Complexes can be Branch

Branch⊂Bank, Some Bank is Office⊂Complex. It is possible that some Complex elements are also Branches. Hence Some Complexes can be Branch — TRUE possibility.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Homes are Flat
No Flat is Floor
Some Floors are Building
All Buildings are House

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: A — Some Floors can be Home

No Flat is Floor means Flat and Floor are completely separate. Homes that are NOT Flat have no restriction from being Floor. Hence Some Floors CAN be Home — TRUE possibility.

questions.q:
Statements:
All Balls are Bat
Some Stumps are Bat
No Stump is Pitch
All Pitches are Green

Which conclusion follows?
opts: Some Stumps are Green is a possibility
All Pitches can never be Ball
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Some Stumps are Green is a possibility

No Stump is Pitch, All Pitches are Green. Green is broader than Pitch. Stumps that avoid Pitch can still be Green (the non-Pitch part). Hence Some Stumps are Green is a possibility — TRUE.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some Wrappers are Cover
All Covers are Toys
Some Gifts are Toys
No Gift is Party

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: B — All Parties can be Cover

No Gift is Party — Parties are separate from Gifts. Cover⊂Toys. No statement links Parties to Covers/Toys, so it is possible for Parties to be Cover. Hence All Parties can be Cover — TRUE possibility.

questions.q:
Statements:
Some barns are tales
Some tales are foxes
All foxes are trays
All trays are cleats

Which conclusions follow?
questions.sol:
Answer: D — Only (a) and (c)

All trays are cleats → Some cleats are trays ✓ (a). All foxes are trays → All trays are cleats → All foxes are cleats ✓ (c). But "All trays are foxes" is NOT given — trays can exist without being foxes ✗ (b). Hence Only (a) and (c).

questions.q:
Statements:
I. All Hide & Seek are Biscuits
II. Some Biscuits are Parle-G
III. No Parle-G is Bread
IV. Some Breads are Britannia

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Some Biscuits are not Bread

No Parle-G is Bread. Some Biscuits are Parle-G → those Parle-G biscuits are NOT Bread. Hence Some Biscuits are not Bread — TRUE (definite conclusion).

questions.q:
Statements:
I. Some 90's are 50's
II. Some 50's are 40's
III. No 40's are 80's
IV. All 80's are 100's

Which conclusion follows?
questions.sol:
Answer: E — Both (c) and (d)

No 40's are 80's, All 80's are 100's. 40's and 100's have no direct link → Some 40's being 100's is possible ✓ (c). 80's and 50's: some 50's are 40's, no 40's are 80's — but 50's that are NOT 40's can be 80's, so All 80's being 50's is possible ✓ (d). Hence Both (c) and (d).

questions.q:
Statements:
I. Some shops are showrooms
II. No showroom is Mall
III. All Malls are Mega mart
IV. Some Mega marts are showroom

Which conclusion follows?
opts: Some Mega marts are not showroom
All Shops can be mega mart
questions.sol:
Answer: D — All follow

(a) Some Mega marts are not showroom: Malls⊂Mega mart, No showroom is Mall → those Mall-Mega marts are not showroom ✓. (b) Some Malls can be shops: No showroom is Mall, Some shops are showrooms — shops that are not showrooms can be Malls ✓. (c) All Shops can be mega mart: possible since no restriction ✓. Hence All follow.
